Terumbu Semakau

Terumbu Semakau

Habitats: Northern edge rocky, central area sandy with some seagrasses, rubbly edges sparsely reefy, denser corals on south-western edge.
1� 12.731’N 103� 46.128’E
21ha, 700m x 400m
(from Google Earth and Earth Point) Conservation status: Does not appear on the URA Master Plan 2008 or the Parks and Waterbodies Plan.
Current conservation activities: The intertidal area is regularly surveyed by wildsingapore. About the name: Terumbu=A reef or rock at low tide but submerged at high water; Semakau=unknown, but semak=scrub or undergrowth. Name probably means “Submerged reef next to Pulau Semakau”.
